title: Conflicting Cues: Who Wins?

runtime.expected_outcome: When channels disagree, the more precise channel dominates the posterior. Precision is the knob for multimodal binding.

symbols.pi_k: channel k's precision (from the G6 precision_vector)

audiences.kid: Your eyes say 'lily pad left' but your ears say 'right'. You trust whichever sense is clearer.
audiences.real: Precision weights determine which channel wins under conflict. Raise visual precision -> vision wins; raise auditory -> hearing wins.
audiences.equation: log q = log prior + sum_k pi_k * log P(o_k | s). Conflicting terms partially cancel; the larger pi_k wins.
audiences.derivation: Precision-weighted inference collapses to a weighted-sum of log-likelihoods, whose variational optimum is a weighted average of channel-specific posteriors (Beierholm et al. 2009).

orchestrate.objective: Demonstrate precision-weighted conflict resolution across two observation channels.
orchestrate.context: frog_pond; sculpt precision_vector so that goal_cue has high precision and tile has low (or vice versa).

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 5.
Beierholm, Quartz & Shams (2009), Bayesian priors are encoded independently.
